<!-- AUTOGENERATED:START - Do not edit between these markers -->
# teams app view

View a Teams app

## Usage

```
teams app view [appId] [options]
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description | Required |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `appId` | App ID | No |

## Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--json <fields>` | [OPTIONAL] Output as JSON with specified fields |
| `--web` | [OPTIONAL] Print the Teams install link |
<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->

## Details

Displays detailed information about a Teams app.

### JSON Output

Use `--json` to output specific fields for scripting:

```bash
teams app view <appId> --json appId,name,endpoint
```

Available fields: `appId`, `teamsAppId`, `name`, `longName`, `version`, `developer`, `shortDescription`, `longDescription`, `websiteUrl`, `privacyUrl`, `termsOfUseUrl`, `endpoint`, `installLink`.

### Install Link

Get the Teams install deep link:

```bash
teams app view <appId> --web
```

### Interactive Mode

Without `[appId]`, launches a searchable app picker that loops — view one app, then pick another or exit.
